Drop-down each step for more information, then click the next button.

################################Checks- Phase##################################

No manual input is required.
DB related parameters and DB space verified here.

Click on the next button.

###############################Preprocessing- Phase##############################

After getting approval from the Application Development Manager, click the radio button to lock the development environment now before proceeding to the next step.

Approval from several teams is required because the landscape has been locked, and no changes will be allowed.

If there are any modifiable state requests in the system, it will display a list of open repairs.
Inform the corresponding team, who will include all changes into the request and release it.
Click on the next button.

DB Clone Phase:

During this phase, the target system (shadow system) is copied from the current system. The Software Update Manager initiates background processes to copy the necessary tables for the shadow system's operation.

Depending on your system and your hardware, this operation can take several hours. These background jobs are executed on the background server that is configured with the BATCH HOST parameter. The number of jobs is determined by the parameter MAX BATCH PROCESSES. It cannot be higher than nine or the number of background processes configured on the background server.
Each table is copied with a single INSERT statement. Therefore, the administrator has to take care that the undo-logs can grow accordingly.

Each cloning job writes a log file called DBCLONE<NUMBER>.<SID>.
At the end of the phase, all errors found in these log files are collected in the DBCLONE.ELG file.
The information in the DBCLONE.ELG file is rather sparse. Therefore, you also have to check the corresponding log files to find detailed information about the error.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

SAPup automatically starts the shadow instance in the START_SHDI_* phases. You can manually start or
stop the shadow instance using the commands SAPup startshd or SAPup stopshd. 
To lock or unlock the shadow instance, use the commands SAPup lockshd or SAPup unlockshd.

To perform SPDD modifications, proceed as follows:

1. The Software Update Manager prompts you to confirm that you want to perform a modification adjustment.
2. Add an entry for the shadow instance to the SAP Logon. For the server and system ID, use the values from the original system
For the instance number, use the value you specified in the preparation units for the shadow instance. The default value is the instance number of the original system plus one.
Since the original system is still running if you use the scenario strategy Standard or Downtime-optimized, you can also log on to the shadow instance in transaction SM59 with the RFC connection SAP_UPGRADE_SHADOW_SYSTEM.

3. Log into the shadow instance as user DDIC with the DDIC password of the original system.
Only the users DDIC and SAP* exist in the shadow instance.
4. To set the system change option, call transaction SE06. Perform the following actions
Set the global setting to Modifiable.
Set the change option for the software components to Modifiable or Restricted modifiable.
Set the SAP namespace to Modifiable.
5. Call transaction SU01 to create one or more users to perform the modification adjustment. The new users exist only on the shadow instance and are not copied to the original system.
Release upgrade only: Copy the user DDIC of client 000. SPS update: Since all users of client 000 are copied in the DBCLONE phase to the shadow instance, any of them can be used for the modification adjustment.
6. Log into the shadow instance with one of the new users. Modification adjustment of ABAP Dictionary objects has to be performed in client 000.
7. To determine the ABAP Dictionary objects that must be adjusted, call transaction SPDD.

 Error related to SPDD.

Applied the resolution (cause-B) available in the 1735879 - Error occurs in phase MAIN_POSTP/UEXP_SPAU or MAIN_POSTP/UEXP_SPDD or MAIN_SHDRUN/UEXP_SPDD_SAV - SAP for Me

Ensure that the following actions are done.

  • Stop all production activities.
  • Ensure that no scheduled batch jobs can start anymore, and await regular completion of currently running jobs
  • Make sure that all users have logged out.
  • Isolate the application server instance (AS instance) as described in the SUM guide, section “isolating the instance’’

Check the radio button Actions completed, then click the next button.

Perform the full DB backup and take backup of SUM directory
Select the radio button Backup completed

Click the next button.

#############################Execution- Phase##################################

Possible Errors in this phase:

1. There is a mismatch in the kernel versions and their patch levels in the ASCS and D00 instances.
During the KX_switch phase, D00 upgraded to the latest kernel from the /usr/sap/SID/SUM/abap/exe directory, but ASCS still had the old kernel executables in the instance specific instance.

Please check the dev_disp log in Drive<Name>: \usr\sap\SID\D<nn>\work
Error: Kernel is not compatible with the system

Please check the kernel version and patch level by executing disp+work in instances like ASCS and PAS by going to path
Drive<Name>:\usr\sap\SID\ASCS<nn>\exe

Drive<Name>: \usr\sap\SID\D<nn>\exe

If any mismatch in Kernel version, then execute the below command in the global profile

Drive<Name>: \usr\sap\SID\SYS\profile: sapcpe pf=<Instance Profile>

Check kernel versions now and restart the application

Resolution: Using the sapcpe command, copy the global kernel executable to the directory appropriate to the ASCS instance.

2. Follow the resolution in the below KBA's

2963518 - FAIL: process igswd.EXE IGS Watchdog not running - SAP for Me

3. Manually move the executables to another location

################################Postprocessing- Phase################################

Downtime:

The time during which you cannot use the system productively.
The upgrade strategy determines when downtime begins during an upgrade.

The duration of the downtime depends on several factors, including:
• Hardware
• Modifications and enhancements of SAP Objects
• Customer developments
• Add-on software
• Integration of Support Packages

Business downtime:  The total time during which the SAP system is not available to end users.

Technical downtime:  The time during which the SAP system is not available due to processing routines in the system launched by the SUM tool.

SUM technical downtime: The time during which the system is not available due to processing during the
SUM runs.

UPGANA File: The report generated upon successful execution additionally contains the time statistics of the performed update, for example, the calculated SUM technical downtime value.

This file will be very useful for taking downtime approval and addressing issues in each phase for the sub-sequent systems in the landscape.

For SUM 1.1: <Drive>:\usr\sap\SID\SUM\abap\doc

For SUM 2.0: <Drive>:\usr\sap\SID\SUM\abap\htdoc\analysis

If you encounter any problems when opening the UPGANA file, please refer to the KBA below.

3412465 - UPGANA.XML not visible (blank page) - SAP for Me

Post-Steps:
1.To release the suspended jobs, execute the report BTCTRNS2 in sa38.

2. Triggering SGEN

Select the check box Regenerate after SAP system upgrade option and click on the components

Select all the components and click on the continue.

Click on the continue button.

Select all the application servers and click on the continue button.

In the event that the system has many applications server's setup, you will get the following screen.

A time out error may occur when the generation set is calculated. You must increase the value of the profile option max_wprun_time, which determines the maximum runtime of a dialog procedure.

In our case Generation set contains 432,668 objects.
Select the job and click the flag button (Execute immediately), which will run the RSPARAGENER8M background job.

You can check the status of the job by using the refresh button.

You need to analyze SM37(RSPARAGENER8M), SM21, ST22 and ST11.

Following the execution of SGEN, you can verify table GENSETM.

  • If the GENSTATUS flag is X, it indicates that the object's generation has been completed.
  • If the GENSTATUS flag is set to S, the object's generation failed due to a syntax errors.
  • If the GENSTATUS flag is E, this indicates that the object's generation failed due to a system error.
  • If you do not see an entry for the object in GENSETC or GENSETM, it signifies that it was not selected during SGEN execution or that a new SGEN execution was initiated.

432,575 Objects generation has been completed.

91 objects generation failed due to syntax error.

Involve the ABAP and functional teams for better understanding.

There are several reasons that cause syntax error. In any case, you need to identify the component of the objects and query the component about whether the syntax error can be ignored.

  • Program does not have a main program defined.
  • Old programs that are not used anymore.
  • Switch linked to the object is off, and program will not be executed until it is turn on.
  • A real syntax error issue.

2 object's generation failed due to a system error.

You need to analyze SM37(Job name: RSPARAGENER8M), SM21, ST22 and ST11.

Possible Errors during the launch of SUM URL:

  1. 3475662 - SUM Installing runtime vc_redist.x64.exe - Installation of vcredist finished with errors - SAP for Me
  2. 2913760 - SUM hangs on screen "Process execution in progress" - SAP for Me
  3. 2322467 - SUM hangs and cannot continue - SAP for Me
  4. 2708098 - SUM UI error: "Forbidden: The user root is not authorized to process the operation ExecuteOperation" - SAP for Me
  5. 2475146 - SUM : logon via SAP Host Agent is failing "Unable to locate the requested resource" or "<hostname>\<sidadm> is not authorized to process the operation ExecuteOperation" - SAP for Me

Possible errors during Extraction Phase:

  1. 3154296 - [ASE Error SQL4601] or DBSL error 99 (db code 4002) in SUM - SAP for Me
  2. If there is a conflict between the Stack.xml content and the download directory

Possible errors during Configuration Phase:

  1. 2594386 - Message "Deviations to calculated level found" in the SUM phase BIND_PATCH - SAP for Me
  2. 2509742 - SUM: PREP_EXTENSION/BIND_PATCH: *WARNING* For some components you have not selected enough support packages - SAP for Me
  3. 1978965 - How to find the equivalent support package for your release - SAP for Me
  4. 2053132 - How to find the import conditions of a Support package or ADD-ON - SAP for Me
  5. 2386898 - Message "Equivalence SP level not reached" received while upgrading/installing an Addon in SAINT - SAP for Me
  6. 1616401 - Parallelism in the Upgrades, EhPs and Support Packages implementations - SAP for Me

Possible errors during Checks Phase:

  1. All DB related errors like
  • DB free Space
  • DB specific parameters
  • DB Patch Update
  • Implementation of Snotes (Depends on the SUM version and patch level. Sometimes it occurs in the extraction and configuration phases.

Possible errors during Pre-processing Phase:

  1. 1901463 - How to unlock the SAP system to perform correction(s) during an upgrade. - SAP for Me
  2. 1790486 - SAP_ABA is in an undefined state that is not safe to be upgraded - SAP for Me
  3. 1972214 - Error during Upgrade: ABAP batch job fails with error "Log writer aborted with RC 1" - SAP for Me
  4. 2530440 - How to configure DIR_PUT in AL11 for the upgrade directory - SAP for Me
  5. Starting and stopping issues with shadow instances.

Possible errors during Execution Phase:

  1. 2963518 - FAIL: process igswd.EXE IGS Watchdog not running - SAP for Me
  2. Startup issues with system.

For any kind of error during update, please refer to the KBA below

2920107 - How to analyze detailed error log files of SUM during the update/upgrade of a NetWeaver ABAP system - SAP for Me

Thank you for reading. If you find this informative, do not forget to hit a like or leave a comment.

 

Thanks,

Mani

entry.title: SAP Developer News March 27th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

PODCAST: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/03/27/sap-developer-news-march-27th-2025/ 

New SAP Enterprise Architecture learning journey 

  • "Discovering SAP Enterprise Architecture" learning journey https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-enterprise-architecture-portfolio  

Good To Know YouTube Shorts on ABAP Cloud 

  • Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQAd1pKjxrPpkOwia5RFw0DE 

What’s new in SAP HANA Cloud Q1 2025  

  • Detailed blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-march-2025/ba-p/14042046  

Cloud Integration and connectivity 

  • EOIO in JMS adapter: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configure-jms-sender-adapter and https://help.sap.com/whats-new/cf0cb2cb149647329b5d02aa96303f56?Valid_as_Of=2025-03-16:2025-03-16&locale=en-US&Component=Cloud+Integration 
  • Generative AI-Based Script Optimization - Groovy scripts: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/generative-ai-based-script-optimization/ba-p/13970403  
  • Cloud Connector 2.18 is out: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-cloud-connector-2-18-is-out/ba-p/14049553  
  • Cloud Connector release notes: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/cf0cb2cb149647329b5d02aa96303f56?Component=Connectivity&locale=en-US&Valid_as_Of=2025-03-06:2025-03-06 

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 New SAP Enterprise Architecture learning journey 

0:59 GoodToKnow YouTube Shorts on ABAP Cloud 

1:41 What’s new in SAP HANA Cloud Q1 2025 

5:21 Cloud Integration and connectivity 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for March 27th, 2025.  

[DJ] I've had many roles in my working life in the SAP Technical Ecosphere. One of those roles was as an SAP Enterprise architect. So when I saw Stefan highlight a new learning journey all about SAP Enterprise architecture, my interest was piqued. As Stefan highlights, it's a core starts, beginners -friendly and covers everything you need to get started with SAP Enterprise Architecture, including what the role entails, fundamentals, key concepts, tools, frameworks and so on, plus a look at SAP's reference architecture. The course is free, so what are you wasting for? Happy learning.  

[Shilpa] Hey, ABAP developers, you might have come across good-to-know YouTube short shots, on ABAP Cloud, which we publish every Friday since the beginning of this year. So far, we have covered 12 videos and there are many more to come. We are covering in two segments. Firstly, about ABAP development tools. If you're new to ADT, these tips and tricks will definitely help you into smooth transitioning into ADT. Secondly, some good facts and points about ABAP cleaner. You can configure ABAP cleaner, which is a tool intended to automate as much as possible with respect to ABAP code style. The link to the playlist is in the description. Check it out and do let us know what do you think.  

[DJ] It is me again, DJ, this time though, standing in for Witalij, my good friend and colleague, because this item is all about SAP Hana Cloud. So there's always innovation in that space. And to mark the end of March, Thomas Hammer has published a blog post highlighting what's new in the SAP Hana Cloud Q1 2025 release. And my goodness me, it's massive. It's a bumper edition. So I thought I'd take some time to summarize the details of the Q1 release, so you know what you have to look forward to. You can get an overview of the main ones in a four-minute video that Thomas links from his blog post, but the detail breakdown you can get from the blog post itself. So Thomas divides up the new. features into different sections. The first one being administration and service management. Basically, there is improved instance management and generation. And there's also better issue management and diagnosis through the automatic summarization of issues. In the machine learning and predictive analytics area, there are facilities to improve machine learning, That's through better regression model building and unified classification. There's also support for text extraction from binary files such as PDF, for example. And there's also a new generative AI toolkit with lots of new features. In the multi-model data processing arena, there's now native support for the resource description framework, RDF, and therefore also sparkle as well as SQL. There's better maximum flow graph problem support. There's also an addition of a new alter vector index statement, which means you can keep your indices up to date, which is great for performance. And there's also support for vector data storage using the native storage extension. In the area of graphical view modeling, There are better facilities for cleaning up intermediate data previews. There's a new facility for using tables views or functions to derive multiple values from input data. There's a new execution hint in the area of currency conversion performance and a new vector embedding function. There's also, last but not least, the ability to target a specific database release when creating calculation views. Beyond all that, there's a ton of innovations also in SAP Hana Cloud Data Lake. There are expanded data availability, data center rather, availability for SAP Cloud and lots of improvements in the area generally of database application development. This was just the briefest of brief summaries. I hope I've done Thomas's blog post justice. I encourage you strongly to go and check out Thomas's blog post and see you next time. 

 [Antonio] Holla, SAP developers. I got some exciting needs to share with you. In Cloud Integration, you can now complete your JMS Queue to either exclusive or non-exclusive access types, meaning that we now have exactly once in order of processing for JMS. With the non-exclusive access type, messages are processed concurrently. With the exclusive access type, messages are processed sequentially by a single consumer and in the order in which they are received. I'm including a link to the documentation in the description. Also, there's a new generative AI feature available in cloud integration. It was announced at SAP Tech at last year. In essence, we're now able to optimize our groovy scripts. The optimized script   functionality will generate a report, which will give you a general assessment of the script. It will also recommend some changes, if applicable, and even provide you with an improved script. Remember that to use this new feature, you will need to have enabled the Gen AI-based features in your tenant. Now let's talk a bit about connectivity. There's a new version of Cloud Connector available. Version 2.18, apart from a couple of bug fixes, there are quite a few enhancements. For example, it now supports Windows Server 2025. There's also support for automatic renewal of the subaccount certificate. It introduces a new centralized management of email and LDAP server trust stores. There's a new troubleshooting section to help you diagnose problems within the Cloud Connector and much more. Make sure to check out the release notes link that's included in the description. Ciao.

entry.title: Automatic Billing Structure
entry.content:
Creating Background Jobs for Billing

In the SAP system, billing documents and credit memos related to orders or deliveries are listed under the "VF04" transaction code. Within the "VF04" screen, all documents awaiting billing are displayed. Billing processes are carried out using the options "Individual Billing Document", "Collective Billing Document", or "Collective Billing Document/Online"

  • In sectors like retail, where invoicing cannot be done individually, the "VF06" transaction code is used to execute mass billing processes in the background.In the "Billing Documents to be Processed" tab, criteria such as "Sales Organization", "Distribution Channel", "Billing Type" and "Sold-to Party" can be specified to save a variant. 

  • In the "Documents to be Selected" tab, "Sales Orders," "Deliveries," "Request for rebate," and "No docs with billing block" can be selected. 


    In the "    Job Started On," the "Start Date" and "Start Time" are selected. Based on the selection made here, the job setup is performed in the background. The "Start Immediately" option starts the job immediately, while the "Test" option displays the details of the job to be set up. No job setup is performed in this case. If neither option is selected, the job is scheduled for the specified date and time and remains in "Planned" status. The selected job name contains "Date" and "Time" information as shown below. Additionally, the number of steps, customers, and documents within the job is also recorded.



    The list of scheduled jobs can be viewed using the transaction code "SM37".



    In the "Job Distribution" section, there are fields for "Number of Jobs" and "Customers per Step". "Number of Jobs" indicates the number of jobs that should run in parallel. "Customers per Step" defines how many customers should be grouped into each step as a batch. In the example shown in the image: 

  • The number of parallel jobs is set to 1.

  • The batch size for processing is 100 customers. 

    If there are 200 customers in the list, only one job will be created in the background, but it will include two steps to process all customers.



    In the "Default Data" section, there are fields for "Billing Type", "Billing Date" and "Pricing Date".These fields are also available in the "Documents to be Billed" tab. When the criteria in the "Documents to be Billed" tab are selected, the relevant documents are filtered.
    When the criteria in the "Default data" section are selected, it means that the values within the selected invoices will be adjusted accordingly. For example, if the "Billing Date" field is set to 10.12.2022, when the selected documents are billed, all of them will be adjusted to have the billing date of 10.12.2022.



    In the "Update" tab, the processing method for the selected billing documents is determined. The options for "Asynchronous" or "Synchronous" can be selected.

    - Asynchronous is more efficient in terms of performance as the job is executed in the background, allowing the process to complete more quickly.

    - Synchronous means the process is executed immediately, but it takes longer to complete.

    For smaller datasets, Synchronous processing can be used. However, for a large number of documents, Asynchronous processing is recommended due to performance considerations.

entry.title: Steps to configure Bitcoin as the functional currency in SAP
entry.content:
A legal entity that prepares its financial statements in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) must adhere to IAS 21 IAS 21 — The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates and IAS 29 IAS 29 — Financial Reporting in Hyperinflationary Economies. These standards mandate the use of a functional and/or reporting currency for financial statements. In SAP, ISO - ISO 4217 — Currency codes are available by default and can be utilized as functional and/or reporting currencies. But cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin are not listed yet!

The following configuration allows Bitcoin to be used as a currency in SAP:

  1. Transaction OY03 - Configure BTC as a currency.
  2. Settings for translating from local currency (EUR) to BTC.
    1. Transaction OBBS - Configure translation ratios.
    2. Transaction OB08 - Maintain exchange rate
  3. Company Code settings
    1. Transaction OX15 - Define internal trading partner and assign BTC as the currency.
    2. Transaction FINSC_LEDGER

Currency Conversion Settings for Company Codes

Company Code Settings for the Ledger 0L

Bitcoin (BTC) is now configured in Accounting and can be used as both a functional and reporting currency. To report balance sheet accounts, vendor accounts, and customer accounts in BTC, the currency type representing BTC must be assigned to the appropriate valuation area using Transaction S_AL0_19000080.

To report fixed assets in BTC, the currency type representing BTC values must be assigned to the depreciation area using Transaction OAYH.

DM&LT offers a currency conversion service to convert systems to new currency target setup. See blog  Data Management and Landscape Transformations - Cu... - SAP Community and Data Unification & Harmonization

With these basic configuration, an entity can report its financial statements in Bitcoin (BTC). However, fully integrating Bitcoin into SAP requires process redesign and enhancements. Consider the following areas:

Purchase-to-Pay

  • Payments to external vendors and affiliated companies in Bitcoin

Hire-to-Retire

  • Employees can be compensated in Bitcoin.
  • Taxes can be paid in Bitcoin.

Order-to-Cash

  • Retailers can accept Bitcoin. How to receive and invoice Bitcoin in SAP 
  • Billing customers in Bitcoin.
  • Clear and settle of open customer items which are paid in Bitcoin.

Record-to-Report
With Bitcoin as an asset in the financial statement, auditors and investors can easily validate Bitcoin balances (UTXO) via their own Bitcoin node or through third-party APIs, such as the mempool.space API. 

To prove ownership of a Bitcoin address, the directors of an legal entity should be able to sign a message. Here are the steps:

  1. Generate a Message: Create a unique message, such as "I own this address [timestamp]".
  2. Sign the Message: Use the private key associated with the Bitcoin address to sign the message. This can be done using wallets like Bitcoin Core, Electrum, or hardware wallets like Ledger and Trezor.
  3. Verify the Signature: Provide the signed message, the signature, and the Bitcoin address to the interested party. They can use the public key to verify the signature and confirm ownership without exposing your private key.

SAP Landscape Transformation SAP Business Data Cloud

Example code for checking a Bitcoin wallet balance via API access:

DATA: lv_url TYPE string,
      lv_address TYPE string VALUE '1A1zP1eP5QGefi2DMPTfTL5SLmv7DivfNa',
      lv_response TYPE string,
      lo_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
      lo_rest_client TYPE REF TO cl_rest_http_client,
      lt_utxos TYPE TABLE OF string,
      lv_utxo TYPE string.

" Define the API endpoint
lv_url = |https://mempool.space/api/address/{ lv_address }/utxo|.

" Create HTTP client instance
CALL METHOD cl_http_client=>create_by_url
  EXPORTING
    url                = lv_url
  IMPORTING
    client             = lo_http_client
  EXCEPTIONS
    argument_not_found = 1
    plugin_not_active  = 2
    internal_error     = 3
    OTHERS             = 4.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Error creating HTTP client'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

" Send the request
CALL METHOD lo_http_client->send
  EXCEPTIONS
    http_communication_failure = 1
    http_invalid_state         = 2
    http_processing_failed     = 3
    OTHERS                     = 4.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Error sending HTTP request'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

" Receive the response
CALL METHOD lo_http_client->receive
  IMPORTING
    data = lv_response
  EXCEPTIONS
    http_communication_failure = 1
    http_invalid_state         = 2
    http_processing_failed     = 3
    OTHERS                     = 4.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  WRITE: / 'Error receiving HTTP response'.
  RETURN.
ENDIF.

" Parse the JSON response
CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_JSON_TO_TABLE'
  EXPORTING
    json = lv_response
  TABLES
    json_table = lt_utxos.

" Output the UTXO details
LOOP AT lt_utxos INTO lv_utxo.
  WRITE: / lv_utxo.
ENDLOOP.

" Free the HTTP client
CALL METHOD lo_http_client->close.

entry.title: Parallel Line As The Next Evolution of Our Public Cloud ERP Product Journey
entry.content:
From Steady Growth to Exponential Success: Parallel Line As The Next Evolution of Our Public Cloud ERP Product Journey 

When we first launched our product in 2017, we set out with the goal of providing a solution that would evolve alongside the needs of our users. Over the years, we witnessed steady, linear growth that reflected our commitment to consistency and quality and a growing business scope. As we moved through the years, customers reflected their demand to evolve, adapt, and think beyond just incremental functional increases. When we embarked onto a new chapter particularly in 2021 and providing to the market starting in 2022, we experienced a pivotal growth spurt that marked a major turning point in our journey. And now, in 2025, we’re excited to introduce another innovation that has the potential to kick it up a notch – the Parallel Line concept – which we believe will be a key to unlocking even more significant flexibility to our customers in the years to come. 

 

A Foundation of Steady Growth

When we first started our journey in 2017, we built our product with a vision of steady, reliable growth. We focused on creating a product that was solid, dependable, and offered clear value to our users for the most essential business processes. As we learned from the feedback of early adopters and refined our offerings, our growth remained steady but linear, reflecting the methodical way we were scaling. This steady pace allowed us to establish a strong base of users, build a team that was focused on innovation, and create a culture that emphasized excellence in every detail.

While the growth was certainly positive, it was clear that our customers will need more than SaaS delivered features. It was obvious that continuously adding capabilities could only take us so far. We needed to fire our next salvo of innovation.

 

2022: A Milestone in Our Growth Trajectory

The year 2022 marked a significant turning point in our growth trajectory. We saw the results of strategic decisions we had been planning for some time, and the introduction of key innovations sparked the growth that would lay the ground for the next level.

One of the most important milestones was the introduction of a 3-system landscape. This new system architecture allowed us to enhance the scalability and flexibility for our customers, making it more adaptable to the evolving needs of our users. This shift was more than just a technical upgrade; it represented a transformation in the way our customers could approach own developments, deployment, and expansion. The 3-system landscape and it´s maintenance schedule allowed our customers to better meet the needs of a growing user base and last-mile-individualization demand.

Alongside this, we also rolled out extended and new extensibility concepts that gave our users, but more importantly our partners more ways to customize and expand the product. By providing greater flexibility and empowering customers to tailor the product to their specific needs, we unlocked a new level of engagement and adoption. With these innovations, growth surged. We observed an increase in new companies joining the community, and existing customers deepened their commitment to the product. But we knew this was just the beginning.

 

Introducing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Parallel Line (PL)

Now, in Q2 2025, we’re thrilled to unveil our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Parallel Line (PL) concept — an entirely new approach to how our customers and partners can manage projects and drive innovation.

PL is not just another feature; it represents a major leap forward in how customers can operate, collaborate, and scale. It’s a new way of thinking about simultaneous project development and allows to manage exploration of new features and production line in parallel without compromising quality or focus.

The PL concept is designed to further increase the addressable market for SaaS ERP and allows customers to handle the increasing complexity of scaling in today’s fast-paced market. By enabling customers to run projects in parallel in an encapsulated tenant, we give flexibility to better address the diverse needs of our customers and help them rapidly respond to new opportunities as they arise.

The Parallel Line is available for 3-system landscape customers and included in their subscription.

The Main line is used for the initial implementation and daily operations to define scope, set up the organizational structure, configure and test business processes and use them productively.

The Parallel line was intensively discussed and tested with selected customers during the Early Adoption phase, and 2 main use cases turned out to be most relevant for our customers:

  1. Make business configuration enhancements and test them safely in a separate environment. The workspace is copied, including all configuration. Once enhancements are completed, they can be merged back into the main line, e.g. for a rollout of new countries/regions or other larger business configuration enhancements.
  2. Leverage the project line as kind of a sandbox system, e.g. to test run data migrations.

 

Customers can initiate a parallel line provisioning via SAP4Me through the 'Branch' step (1). Branch is a function that is used to set up the parallel line and replicate the existing configuration of the main line to the parallel line. ‘Rebase’ (2) is a function that is used to send any configurations created in the main line to the parallel line after branch. To complete the cycle, ‘Merge’ (3) is a function that is used to send any business configuration changes from the parallel line to the main line into production, once ready.

For those eager to delve into more details, the Digital Library (accessible with any S-user) and Notes 3580675 and 3542536 provide comprehensive overviews of the parallel line.

This is an update on how far we’ve come. PL is designed to be the next catalyst for growth – allowing our customers to expand, scale, and innovate more rapidly than ever before.

 

Wrap Up

With the introduction of Parallel Line and the continued evolution of our 3-system landscape and extensibility features, we are poised for even more significant growth. But we aren’t just focused on scaling – we’re focused on ensuring that each innovation we introduce aligns with our core values of quality, reliability, and customer empowerment.

We are committed to continuing our journey with the same passion and drive that got us here in the first place. The road ahead is full of opportunities, and with the Parallel Line, we’re more equipped than ever to navigate it.

 

Final Thoughts

What started as a steady, linear growth trajectory has transformed into something far more dynamic and exciting. The innovations we’ve introduced over the past few years – from the 3-system landscape to extensibility concepts and now PL – have not only fueled our product’s growth but have also positioned us to lead in an increasingly competitive market. With PL, we are now ready to enter a new era of growth and innovation.

For those eager to read more, the following “How-To” Practitioner blogs have been released.

Parallel Line Branch via SAP for ME

Introduction to the Parallel Line - SAP Community

And further details can be found here: Set Up Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

Thanks for tuning in, looking forward to your thoughts

Bert

entry.title: The Death of Traditional ERP?
entry.content:
The Death of Traditional ERP?


For decades, ERP systems have been the backbone of enterprise operations, managing everything from finance and supply chain to HR and procurement. But as technology evolves, traditional monolithic ERP models are being challenged by more flexible, connected, and AI-driven solutions.

Is this the end of traditional ERP as we know it? Or is it simply evolving?

 Why Traditional ERP is Fading

Legacy ERP systems have long suffered from:
❌Rigid architectures that make upgrades costly and time-consuming.
❌Siloed data structures that limit cross-functional insights.
❌High implementation costs that deter smaller enterprises from adoption.

In today’s fast-moving business environment, companies need agility, real-time insights, and AI-driven decision-making—capabilities that traditional on-premise ERP often lacks.

 The Rise of Composable and Cloud-Based ERP

Modern enterprises are shifting to composable, cloud-based architectures that allow greater flexibility and scalability. SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a prime example of how ERP is transforming:

✅Modular and composable architecture – Companies can implement only the components they need.
✅Real-time data processing – Leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud, organizations can unify data sources for better analytics.
✅AI-Embedded Workflows – With tools like SAP Joule, ERP is no longer just transactional but predictive and automated.

How Low-Code and AI Are Reshaping ERP

The shift away from traditional ERP is further accelerated by:

🔹Low-code and no-code platforms like SAP Build, allowing business users to develop applications without heavy IT involvement.
🔹AI-driven automation through SAP Business AI, enabling proactive decision-making rather than reactive problem-solving.
🔹Connected ecosystems with SAP Integration Suite, allowing ERP to seamlessly integrate with other enterprise systems.

 What’s Next? ERP’s Evolution, Not Its End

ERP is not disappearing—it is transforming into a smarter, more connected digital core. Companies must:

✅Embrace cloud and AI to make ERP more intelligent.
✅Adopt a modular approach for greater flexibility.
✅Leverage real-time data for proactive business insights.

So, is traditional ERP dead? Not quite. It’s simply evolving into something more powerful. The real question is: Is your business evolving with it?

What’s your take? Is the ERP model as we know it coming to an end?

entry.title: Implementing Effective Audit Controls in SAP(Part1- Basis)
entry.content:
Implementing Effective Audit Controls in SAP  (Part1- Basis)

Audit compliance in SAP applications is not just about meeting regulatory requirements; it's about building a robust, secure, and efficient system that supports the organization's overall goals and objectives

There will be a series of audit, compliance and best practices headway for customers to consider, implement and strengthen the SAP ecosystem.

This is the first blog on SAP technical controls, next on Authorization and last on Cloud audit controls with automation insights.

Let’s get started!!  

Why We Need Audit Compliance in SAP Applications
Trust and Credibility:
1. Stakeholder Confidence: Compliance with audit standards builds trust among stakeholders, including customers, investors, and partners, by demonstrating that the organization is well-managed and reliable.
2. Reputation Management: Maintaining audit compliance helps protect and enhance the organization's reputation by showing commitment to ethical practices and transparency.

Legal and Regulatory Requirements:
1. Avoiding Penalties: Non-compliance can lead to significant fines and legal consequences. Regular audits ensure that the organization stays compliant with relevant laws and regulations.
2. Preparedness for External Audits: Internal audit compliance prepares the organization for external audits, making the process smoother and less disruptive.

Operational Excellence:
1. Continuous Improvement: Audit compliance encourages a culture of continuous improvement, where processes are regularly reviewed and optimized.
2. Risk Mitigation: Identifying and addressing risks proactively through audits helps in maintaining operational stability and resilience.

Financial Health:
1. Accurate Financial Management: Ensuring that financial records are accurate and compliant helps in better financial planning and management.
2. Investor Confidence: Reliable financial reporting and compliance attract and retain investors by showcasing the organization's financial health and stability

Illustrating SAP Basis and Security audit controls through a whitepaper, beneficial for life sciences and healthcare customers, as well as other industries.

Encourage readers to adopt best practices and utilize available resources to enhance their SAP system security.

Platform Level Access Control:

OS-Level Access Control

  • User Authentication: Implement OS authentication process or workflow to ensure that only authorized users can access the system on-premises or hyperscale's.
  • User Accounts: Create and manage user accounts with appropriate permissions using utility like useradd and passwd on UNIX/Linux or Computer Management on Windows.
  • Access Control Lists (ACLs): Set file and directory permissions to restrict access to sensitive files. Use commands like chmod, chown, and chgrp on UNIX/Linux systems.
  • Group Policies: Implement group policies on platform to manage user permissions and access rights.

DB-Level Access Control

  • User Management: Create database user accounts with specific roles and privileges. For example, you can define user attributes such as authentication method, default tablespace, and user profile
  • Roles and Privileges: Assign roles and privileges to control the types of SQL statements users can execute and the data they can access
  • Auditing and Monitoring: Monitor and record user actions on the database, including those performed by administrators. This helps in detecting and preventing unauthorized access.
  • Security Policies: Establish and enforce security policies to protect the database from accidental or malicious destruction of data. Password policies implement.

Regular Reviews:

  • Conduct Monthly / quarter reviews of user access to both the OS and DB to ensure that permissions are up-to-date and appropriate. Perform user comparison on servers.
  • All new users should have associated requests in incident management and approval.
  • Users offboarded from Company or project, personnel access removed from platform.
  • Regular review the access or modification of DB user’s privilege.
  • Annual reset the system/service user passwords to stay compliance. 

User access to third-party e.g Batch scheduler

Identify Users and Access Levels

  • List Users: Compile a list of all users who have access to the third-party applications.
  • Access Levels: Document the access levels and permissions assigned to each user.
  • Current Roles: Verify that the access levels are appropriate for the users' current roles and responsibilities.

Regular Reviews:

  • Scheduled Reviews: Conduct regular access reviews, such as quarterly or bi-annually, to ensure that access permissions are up-to-date.
  • De-provisioning: Remove access for users who no longer need it, such as those who have changed roles or left the organization

Secure Database Connections:

Securing database connections in SAP is crucial for protecting sensitive data and ensuring compliance with security standards.

Ensure Secure Network Communications

  • Use Firewall to secure inbound and outbound communications.
  • Configure SSL/TLS to secure the connection between the SAP system and the database. This involves setting up certificates and enabling SSL/TLS in the database configuration. 

Implement Strong Authentication Mechanisms

  • Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): Implement RBAC to ensure that users have the minimum necessary permissions to perform their tasks.

Enable Connectivity Logging:

  • Enable logging on oracle or HANA database to generate incoming Server name / IPs and connection string visible connection users.

 

on HANA

Transaction Code HDBSQL: Use HDBSQL to enable and configure trace settings for the HANA database.

Trace Levels: Set appropriate trace levels to capture detailed information about incoming connections.

Elevated access and user switch to <sid>adm and <DB>user 

  • Ensuring a strict process for root and administrator access is crucial for maintaining security and compliance.
  • Root access can only be granted traversing approval workflow from designated authority.
  • Elevated access should be temporary access or time-bound access.
  • Platform should have a logging enables to record root, admin, user sessions for commands getting executed.

Regular Reviews:

  • Enable and regularly review connectivity logs to validate the connections and detect any unauthorized activities.
  • All user switch to SAP technical users should be validated for commands and transactions e.g ALTER/DELETE to database tables.

Client opening: 

Enable Change Logging

  • Transaction Code SCC4: Use this transaction to configure client settings and enable change logging for client-specific activities.
  • Profile Parameters: Ensure that profile parameters like rsau/enable and rsau/selection_slots are correctly configured to capture relevant events.
  • Maintain detailed documentation of all client opening activities, including approvals and justifications
  • Access Controls: Implement strict access controls to ensure that only authorized personnel with emergency access (firefighter) can open or modify clients with change management.

Regular reviews:

  • Perform monthly review on client open change logs.
  • Match the documented changes and Firefighter access.
  • Review the SM20 security audit logs for the tasks performed with client opening.

Business Batch Jobs:

Controlling batch job modifications and failures is essential for maintaining operational efficiency and ensuring compliance during audits.

Implement Change Control Procedures

  • Approval Workflow: Establish a formal approval workflow for any modifications to batch jobs. Ensure that changes are reviewed and approved by authorized personnel before implementation.
  • Documentation: Maintain detailed documentation of all changes, including the rationale, approval, and implementation detail.
  • Report: Generate a customize report from SAP on table TBTCO/TBTCP on batch Creation,Modification,Deletion,Suspension or if using third party, schedule report to generate same.
  • Failed batch jobs handling: Implement robust error reporting mechanisms to capture and log job failures. Ensure that errors are documented and analyzed for root cause.

Ideas:

1- you can give priority to jobs based on business criticality.

2- Inimitable job naming conventions for each modules include priority and frequency.

3- Set the code-red process for high priority jobs.

4- Automate or integrate Batch failures with incident management to route responsible team.

Regular Reviews:

  1. Perform half yearly / annual review of batch job changes.
  2. Monthly review the failed batch jobs to be in control on root cause and preventive measures

SAP Parameters

  • Auditing control of SAP parameters is essential for ensuring system security and compliance.
  • Enable Security Audit Log: Use this transaction to configure the Security Audit Log. Set up filters to capture relevant events, including changes to SAP parameter
  • PAHI Table: Any changes to parameter should be visible in PAHI table and reviewed frequently.
  • Documentation: Maintain detailed documentation of all changes, including the rationale, approval, and implementation detail.

Regular Reviews:

  • PAHi table to be reviewed and vet with changes implemented along with approval.
  • User session logs on platform to be review for any profile changes on production.
  • As much as possible all profile to be arranged in alphabetical order and changes to perform from RZ10/RZ11 SAP (avoid changes from platform).

Change Management:

Auditing and controlling change management in SAP is crucial for ensuring system integrity, minimizing risks, and maintaining compliance

  • Formal Change Requests: Use a standardized process for submitting, reviewing, and approving change requests. Ensure that all changes are documented and tracked in CAB (Change authority board)
  • Approval Workflow: Establish a formal approval workflow involving multiple stakeholders to review and approve change. e.g.. Use SAP Solution Manager's ChaRM to manage and track changes across the SAP landscape. ChaRM provides tools for planning, documenting, and approving changes.
  • Quality Gate Management: Implement Quality Gate Management to ensure that changes are thoroughly tested and validated before moving to production.
  • Access Controls: Implement strict access controls to ensure that only authorized personnel can promote changes to production.
  • Documentation: Maintain detailed documentation of all changes, including approvals and justifications.
  • Access Control: STMS restricted for business and IT users. SAP Basis should only allow imports to perform. To some customers where ChARM implemented, SAP Basis only allows to import from Charm in production and STMS limited for display.

Regular Reviews:

  • Conduct regular audits to ensure that changes are implemented according to the approved process and comply with organizational policies.
  • Transport has been imported in production with authorized personnel.
  • DBTABLOG: Review of DBTABLOG which holds log changes made to database tables. This table is useful for auditing and tracking modifications to ensure data integrity and compliance.
  • CDHDR : Review of change document header table, storing information about changes made to business objects

Thanks for the read. Next blog on Authorization Control to publish shortly. 

Cheers, 

VS

entry.title: Why You Should Avoid Directly Creating VA01 Sales Orders and Embrace SAP CPQ - Improve Sales Process
entry.content:
The Limitations of Directly Creating VA01 Sales Orders in ERP, S/4 HANA

Creating sales orders directly using VA01 involves manually entering data, which can be time-consuming and prone to errors. This method often lacks integration with other systems, leading to discrepancies in pricing, product configurations, and customer information, profitability calculations, guardrails, negotiations & output document acceptance from customer.

Additionally, it does not provide a guided sales process, which can result in inconsistent handling of sales opportunities .

Hybrid Business Process flow using SAP CPQ to maximize your Deals

In today's fast-paced business environment, efficiency and accuracy are paramount. One area where companies often struggle is in your sales order processes. Traditionally, many organizations have relied on directly creating sales orders using VA01 in SAP ERP or S4 HANA. While this method is straightforward, it can lead to inefficiencies and errors. Instead, leveraging a hybrid solution via SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) can significantly streamline the Quote to Cash or Lead to Cash processes and available with SAP Standard Integrations offerings.

Bundled Products ( Sales, Service, Subscriptions Products) Scenario - Solution Quotation

Bundled Products ( Sales, Service, Subscriptions Products) Scenario - Solution Order

The Advantages of Using SAP CPQ

SAP CPQ offers a robust solution to these challenges by providing a centralized platform for managing the entire sales process, from quoting to order fulfillment.

Here are some key benefits:

  1. Increased Efficiency and Productivity: SAP CPQ streamlines the sales process by eliminating manual data entry, saving time and increasing productivity .
  2. Improved Quote Accuracy: Integration with product master data ensures that all quotes are accurate and up-to-date, reducing errors and improving customer satisfaction .
  3. Shorter Sales Cycles: The guided sales process helps sales representatives close deals more quickly, shortening sales cycles and boosting revenue generation .
  4. Enhanced Customer Experience: SAP CPQ provides a self-service portal for configuring products, requesting quotes, and tracking orders, improving the overall customer experience.

SAP CPQ - Business Process flow for Back-end Integ... - SAP Community

Comprehensive Integration Capabilities

SAP CPQ enables seamless integration with various systems, enhancing the overall sales process:

  • CRM Integration: SAP CPQ integrates with your CRM systems, allowing opportunities to be converted into quotes effortlessly.
  • SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing: Integration with SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing simplifies complex product configurations and ensures accurate pricing for the cart in real-time .
  • Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM): SAP CPQ integrates with CLM systems, streamlining the contract creation and approval process .
  • Output Documents and Approvals: Automated generation of output documents and streamlined approval workflows ensure consistency and compliance .
  • Standard Integration Packages via CPI: SAP CPQ offers standard integration packages via SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) for sales orders, solution quotations, and solution orders

SAP CPQ - Business Process flow for Back-end Integ... - SAP Community

How SAP CPQ Integrates with SAP S/4HANA

SAP CPQ seamlessly integrates with SAP S/4HANA, enabling the transfer of pricing and product configuration data, and master data replication

This integration ensures that all sales information is accurate and up-to-date, providing a consistent and efficient sales process.

Business Process Flow Diagram

Architecture Views | Solution Business for Hybrid Deployment | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Architecture Views | Lead to Cash for Hybrid Deployment | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Architecture Views | Network-Based Sales Processing for Hybrid Deployment | SAP Business Accelerator Hub


SAP CPQ - SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud Integration

Conclusion

By avoiding the direct creation of VA01 sales orders and adopting a hybrid solution via SAP CPQ, organizations can significantly enhance their sales efficiency, accuracy, and customer satisfaction. Embracing this modern approach not only streamlines the sales process but also positions businesses for greater success in today's competitive landscape. Also it ensures you have clear auditing track of the entire Quote to Cash process been automated end to end.

Are you ready to transform your sales process with SAP CPQ? Let's start the journey towards a more efficient and productive sales operation!
